name: Anjney Midha
thesis_notes: Believes foundation models are the next platform shift, comparable to mobile and cloud
Focuses on companies building at the model layer, not just application layer
Has publicly stated that compute-efficient architectures will win over brute-force scaling
Prioritizes teams with deep research backgrounds and novel technical approaches

recent_relevant_deals.relevance: Foundation model lab, similar stage trajectory

public_statements_on_world_models: Has referenced 'world simulation' as one of the most promising frontiers beyond language models in podcast appearances
Tweeted about the importance of 3D understanding for AGI development
known_portfolio_conflicts: Pika (generative video, partial overlap on visual generation)
Character.ai (different modality but consumes similar talent pool)
warm_intro_paths: Elad Gil has co-invested with Anjney on multiple deals (Pika, others)
Garry Tan (YC CEO, Odyssey angel) has strong a16z relationships

name: Feyza Haskaraman
thesis_notes: Focuses on AI infrastructure and enterprise AI applications
Interested in companies that build durable technical moats through proprietary data or architectures
Values API-first business models that enable platform ecosystems
Has expressed interest in spatial computing and 3D content generation
public_statements_on_world_models: Has discussed the importance of multimodal understanding beyond text at industry events
Published notes on spatial AI as an emerging investment category
warm_intro_paths: DCVC (existing Odyssey investor) has co-invested with Felicis on deep-tech deals
Jeff Dean (Odyssey angel, former Google AI lead) has broad Silicon Valley network connections

name: Nathan Benaich
thesis_notes: Publishes the annual 'State of AI Report', the most widely cited AI industry analysis
Deep conviction in world models and simulation as a core AI frontier
Backs companies where the founding team has top-tier research credentials
Prefers capital-efficient approaches to model training

recent_relevant_deals.relevance: Existing investor, deep context on the company
World models for autonomous driving (Jeff Hawke's former company)

public_statements_on_world_models: 2025 State of AI Report featured a dedicated section on world models as 'the next frontier beyond language'
Has publicly praised Odyssey's approach to learning from real-world data capture
Tweeted: 'World models that learn from physical reality, not just internet data, will be the ones that generalize'
warm_intro_paths: Direct relationship. Board observer or advisor likely.

name: EQT Ventures Team
thesis_notes: European-headquartered but globally active, strong presence in frontier AI
Led Odyssey's $18M Series A in November 2024
Thesis on 'transformative technology' with particular focus on AI infrastructure
Values capital efficiency and technical differentiation over pure scale
public_statements_on_world_models: Investment memo for Odyssey likely positioned world models as the 'next compute paradigm' (inferred from the investment thesis)
EQT Ventures blog highlighted Odyssey as a portfolio highlight in their 2025 review
warm_intro_paths: Direct relationship. Lead investor with board seat.

name: Elad Gil
stage_focus: Seed through Growth (personal checks at every stage)
thesis_notes: Author of 'High Growth Handbook', the canonical guide to scaling startups
One of Silicon Valley's most prolific angel investors in AI, with 40+ AI company investments
Former VP at Twitter, led Google's mobile and corporate strategy
Looks for 'category-defining' technical approaches, not incremental improvements
public_statements_on_world_models: Has discussed the importance of 'understanding the physical world' in AI development on his blog
Publicly noted that world models represent a necessary step toward general intelligence
known_portfolio_conflicts: Invested in many AI companies but typically non-competitive positions as an angel
